title: Question: String parameter access in C++
body:
---


Author Name: **Dennis Muhlestein**
Original Redmine Message: 1806 from https://www.veripool.org



---

I'm looking to pass the contents of a string paramter on a module to a c helper class.  Is this possible?

```
module t #(parameter TEST="Hello")
(
....
    reg output = $c("mClass->do_something(", TEST, ")' );

);

`systemc_header
class mClass
... snip snip

  bool do_something ( const char* test ) {
     ...
  }

```

I'm not sure what the best way to pass a string parameter to a function is.  i.e., how do you convert the parameter data to string format?
